items.title: Undercover in Laos: how Chinese tourism fuels animal trafficking – video
items.summary: Chinese tourism is booming in Laos and the illegal wildlife trade is booming with it. Pangolin scales, rhino horn and elephant ivory are all being sold at secret shops and restaurants as a new high-speed rail line brings millions of visitors to the country.
items.audio: audio/Undercover in Laos_ how Chinese tourism fuels anim.mp3

items.title: Can humans hibernate their way to Mars?
items.summary: Scientists are trying to recreate the biology that lets animals survive months without food or water, in hopes of making deep-space travel possibleLong-term space travel is bad for your health. Very bad. Being in space exposes humans to dangerously high levels of radiation; extended exposure to microgravity can damage a range of organ systems, including muscles, bones and eyes.
items.audio: audio/Can humans hibernate their way to Mars.mp3

items.title: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo is dead. How long will ICE be allowed to hide its face?
items.summary: A government that demands instant obedience while concealing its identity cannot expect trust – or escape responsibility for the consequencesThere are videos that do more than document a story. They alter the way we understand it.Ronaldo Salgado spent 7 July searching for his father. At the scene of a shooting in Houston’s East End, he found his father’s work van behind police tape.
items.audio: audio/Lorenzo Salgado Araujo is dead_ How long will ICE .mp3

items.title: They Fight review – boxing drama is an emotional gutpunch
items.summary: Anchored by an indelible André Holland performance, the film finds tenderness and warmth amid its gritty Washington DC backdropIn the lineage of Creed and Million Dollar Baby, They Fight makes yet another compelling case for why boxing remains a timeless allegory for the human condition. This time it’s Walt (André Holland) who’s staring up at a 10-count.
items.audio: audio/They Fight review _ boxing drama is an emotional g.mp3
